contentBlocks.original:
महीसुतो महाभागो मङ्गलो मङ्गलप्रदः ।
महावीरो महाशूरो महाबलपराक्रमः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahīsutō mahābhāgō maṅgaḻō maṅgaḻapradaḥ ।
mahāvīrō mahāśūrō mahābalaparākramaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mahīsuta (son of the earth), Mahābhāga (of great fortune), Maṅgala (the auspicious), Maṅgalaprada (bestower of auspiciousness), Mahāvīra (great hero), Mahāśūra (great warrior), Mahābalaparākrama (of mighty strength and prowess). (1)

contentBlocks.original:
महारौद्रो महाभद्रो माननीयो दयाकरः ।
मानदोऽमर्षणः क्रूरस्तापपापविवर्जितः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāraudrō mahābhadrō mānanīyō dayākaraḥ ।
mānadō'marṣaṇaḥ krūrastāpapāpavivarjitaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mahāraudra (greatly fierce), Mahābhadra (greatly auspicious), Mānanīya (worthy of honour), Dayākara (compassionate), Mānadā (bestower of honour), Amarṣaṇa (intolerant of offence), Krūra (fierce), Tāpapāpavivarjita (free from affliction and sin). (2)

contentBlocks.original:
सुप्रतीपः सुताम्राक्षः सुब्रह्मण्यः सुखप्रदः ।
वक्रस्तम्भादिगमनो वरेण्यो वरदः सुखी ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
supratīpaḥ sutāmrākṣaḥ subrahmaṇyaḥ sukhapradaḥ ।
vakrastambhādigamanō varēṇyō varadaḥ sukhī ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Supratīpa (of handsome form), Sutāmrākṣa (of bright copper-red eyes), Subrahmaṇya (most excellent), Sukhaprada (giver of happiness), Vakrastambhādigamana (one who moves in retrograde, stationary, etc.), Vareṇya (the most excellent), Varada (bestower of boons), Sukhī (joyful). (3)

contentBlocks.original:
वीरभद्रो विरूपाक्षो विदूरस्थो विभावसुः ।
नक्षत्रचक्रसञ्चारी क्षत्रपः क्षात्रवर्जितः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vīrabhadrō virūpākṣō vidūrasthō vibhāvasuḥ ।
nakṣatrachakrasañchārī kṣatrapaḥ kṣātravarjitaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vīrabhadra (auspicious hero), Virūpākṣa (of uncommon eyes), Vidūrastha (dwelling afar), Vibhāvasu (of brilliant wealth), Nakṣatracakrasañcārī (moving through the wheel of nakṣatras), Kṣatrapa (protector of the warrior class), Kṣātravarjita (beyond the warrior class). (4)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षयवृद्धिविनिर्मुक्तः क्षमायुक्तो विचक्षणः ।
अक्षीणफलदः चक्षुर्गोचरः शुभलक्षणः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣayavṛddhivinirmuktaḥ kṣamāyuktō vichakṣaṇaḥ ।
akṣīṇaphaladaḥ chakṣurgōcharaḥ śubhalakṣaṇaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kṣayavṛddhivinirmukta (free from waning and waxing), Kṣamāyukta (endowed with forbearance), Vichakṣaṇa (discerning), Akṣīṇaphalada (giver of inexhaustible fruits), Cakṣurgocara (perceptible to the eye), Śubhalakṣaṇa (of auspicious marks). (5)

contentBlocks.original:
वीतरागो वीतभयो विज्वरो विश्वकारणः ।
नक्षत्रराशिसञ्चारो नानाभयनिकृन्तनः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vītarāgō vītabhayō vijvarō viśvakāraṇaḥ ।
nakṣatrarāśisañchārō nānābhayanikṛntanaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vītarāga (free from passion), Vītabhaya (free from fear), Vijvara (free from fever), Viśvakāraṇa (cause of the universe), Nakṣatrarāśisañcāra (moving through the nakṣatras and rāśis), Nānābhayanikṛntana (cutter of many fears). (6)

contentBlocks.original:
कमनीयो दयासारः कनत्कनकभूषणः ।
भयघ्नो भव्यफलदो भक्ताभयवरप्रदः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kamanīyō dayāsāraḥ kanatkanakabhūṣaṇaḥ ।
bhayaghnō bhavyaphaladō bhaktābhayavarapradaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kamanīya (pleasing), Dayāsāra (essence of compassion), Kanatkanakabhūṣaṇa (adorned with shining gold ornaments), Bhayaghna (destroyer of fear), Bhavyaphalada (giver of excellent fruits), Bhaktābhayavaraprada (bestower of fearlessness and boons to devotees). (7)

contentBlocks.original:
शत्रुहन्ता शमोपेतः शरणागतपोषकः ।
साहसः सद्गुणाऽध्यक्षः साधुः समरदुर्जयः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śatruhantā śamōpētaḥ śaraṇāgatapōṣakaḥ ।
sāhasaḥ sadguṇā'dhyakṣaḥ sādhuḥ samaradurjayaḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śatruhantā (destroyer of enemies), Śamopeta (endowed with peace), Śaraṇāgatapōṣaka (nourisher of those who take refuge), Sāhasa (valiant), Sadguṇādhyakṣa (overseer of all virtues), Sādhu (saintly), Samaradurjaya (invincible in battle). (8)

contentBlocks.original:
दुष्टदूरः शिष्टपूज्यः सर्वकष्टनिवारकः ।
दुश्चेष्टवारको दुःखभञ्जनो दुर्धरो हरिः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
duṣṭadūraḥ śiṣṭapūjyaḥ sarvakaṣṭanivārakaḥ ।
duśchēṣṭavārakō duḥkhabhañjanō durdharō hariḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Duṣṭadūra (far from the wicked), Śiṣṭapūjya (worthy of worship by the righteous), Sarvakaṣṭanivāraka (remover of all hardships), Duśceṣṭavāraka (preventer of evil deeds), Duḥkhabhañjana (destroyer of sorrow), Durdhara (hard to restrain), Hari (the remover). (9)

contentBlocks.original:
दुःस्वप्नहन्ता दुर्धर्षो दुष्टगर्वविमोचकः ।
भरद्वाजकुलोद्भूतो भूसुतो भव्यभूषणः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
duḥsvapnahantā durdharṣō duṣṭagarvavimōchakaḥ ।
bharadvājakulōdbhūtō bhūsutō bhavyabhūṣaṇaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Duḥsvapnahantā (destroyer of bad dreams), Durdharṣa (hard to overcome), Duṣṭagarvavimocaka (liberator from the pride of the wicked), Bharadvājakulodbhūta (born from the lineage of Bharadvāja), Bhūsuta (son of the earth), Bhavyabhūṣaṇa (ornament of the auspicious). (10)

Raktāmbara (wearing red robes), Raktavapu (of red body), Bhaktapālanatatpara (ever devoted to protecting devotees), Caturbhuja (four-armed), Gadādhārī (mace-bearer), Meṣavāha (riding a ram), Amitāśana (of boundless appetite). (11)

Śaktiśūladhara (bearing śakti-spear and trident), Śakta (powerful), Śastravidyāviśārada (adept in the science of weapons), Tārkika (the logician), Tāmasādhāra (support of the dark realm), Tapasvī (the ascetic), Tāmralocana (of copper-red eyes). (12)

Taptakāñcanasaṅkāśa (resembling molten gold), Raktakiñjalkasannibha (like red lotus pollen), Gotrādhideva (presiding deity of the mountains), Gomadhyacara (moving in the Taurus / cow-constellation), Guṇavibhūṣaṇa (adorned with virtues). (13)

Asṛgaṅgāraka (Aṅgāraka of blood-red hue), Avantīdeśādhīśa (lord of the Avantī country), Janārdana (stirrer of men), Sūryayāmyapradēśastha (stationed in the southern zone from the Sun), Yauvana (ever youthful), Yāmyadiṅmukha (facing the southern direction). (14)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकोणमण्डलगतस्त्रिदशाधिपसन्नुतः ।
शुचिः शुचिकरः शूरो शुचिवश्यः शुभावहः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikōṇamaṇḍalagatastridaśādhipasannutaḥ ।
śuchiḥ śuchikaraḥ śūrō śuchivaśyaḥ śubhāvahaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Trikoṇamaṇḍalagata (established in the triangular orb), Tridaśādhipasannuta (praised by the lord of the thirty gods), Śuci (pure), Śucikara (purifier), Śūra (heroic), Śucivaśya (moved by purity), Śubhāvaha (bringer of auspiciousness). (15)

contentBlocks.original:
मेषवृश्चिकराशीशो मेधावी मितभाषणः ।
सुखप्रदः सुरूपाक्षः सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mēṣavṛśchikarāśīśō mēdhāvī mitabhāṣaṇaḥ ।
sukhapradaḥ surūpākṣaḥ sarvābhīṣṭaphalapradaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Meṣavṛścikārāśīśa (lord of Aries and Scorpio), Medhāvī (intelligent), Mitabhāṣaṇa (of measured speech), Sukhaprada (giver of happiness), Surūpākṣa (of beautiful eyes), Sarvābhīṣṭaphalaprada (giver of all desired fruits). (16)
Thus ends the Śrī Aṅgāraka Aṣṭottaraśatanāma Stotram.
